source_jobs.title: Satellite Laser Ranging Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are Foundational.

Foundational is an early-stage, mission-driven startup solving one of the hardest engineering challenges of the decade: creating an exquisite, real-time understanding of Earth and orbit using ground-based lasers.

We aren't just gathering data; we are building the platform that will enable the next generation of space traffic control, resilient navigation, and Earth observation. - essential for the sustainability and resilience of the space domain, monitoring our climate and empowering the future economy.

This mission, this technology... will be foundational to the future of civilization.



The Role

As our core expert in optical tracking, you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of our SLR (Satellite Laser Ranging) and Space Debris Laser Ranging (SDLR) systems. You will bridge the gap between optical design, hardware integration, and the software algorithms required to close the link budget.

What You'll Be Doing

 - System Modelling & Simulation: creating comprehensive models for SLR and SDLR performance, including radiometric link budgets, atmospheric transmission, and detector probability of detection.

 - R&D of Novel Systems: Leading the design and integration of next-gen laser ranging architectures, selecting components (pulsed lasers, single-photon detectors, optics), and overseeing the assembly of optical benches.

 - Advanced Data Processing: Developing novel algorithms to process raw time-of-flight data, filtering noise from background photons, and refining orbital determination accuracy.

 - Deployment & Validation: managing the installation of optical ground stations and conducting rigorous Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) to validate ranging precision and pointing accuracy.

 - Laser Safety Leadership: Acting as a subject matter expert for laser safety, performing risk assessments, and ensuring compliance with UK/EU standards (e.g., BS EN 60825) for outdoor laser propagation.
   
   

Essential Experience:

 - Domain Expertise: Demonstrated experience designing, building, or operating SLR stations, Optical Ground Stations (OGS), or atmospheric LiDAR systems.

 - Atmospheric Physics: A strong understanding of laser beam propagation through turbulent media, including beam wander, scintillation, and the use of link budgets to predict system return rates.

 - Signal Processing: Proficiency in signal processing techniques specific to LiDAR/SLR (e.g., constant fraction discriminators, range gate optimization, and noise filtering algorithms).

 - Timing Systems: Hands-on experience with high-precision timing hardware (Event Timers, GNSS synchronization, PTP/NTP protocols) and an understanding of jitter/latency sources.

 - Software Stack: Strong proficiency in Python for instrument control (drivers, serial comms) and data analysis (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas). Experience with Linux environments is mandatory.

 - Astronomy Hardware: Familiarity with telescope mounts (equatorial/Alt-Az), star tracking, and pointing models.

 - Work Status: Must have the right to work in the UK.

 - Proactive & Self-Motivated: You thrive in a startup environment where requirements evolve, and you are comfortable working with autonomy.
   
   

Bonus Points (We'd love it if you...)

 - Experience with FPGA programming for timing applications.

 - Familiarity with orbital mechanics (TLEs, CPF/CRD formats).

 - Experience with optical design software (Zemax or Code V).
   

Why Join Us?

 - A high-impact role with genuine ownership and autonomy in a new startup

 - Government backed EMI scheme options in a fast-growing VC backed startup operating in stealth.

 - Dynamic and collaborative work environment: We foster a culture of innovation, creativity, and teamwork.

 - Make a real impact: Your contributions will directly shape our product and company success.

 - Opportunity to work on cutting-edge technology: Use your skills on meaningful, challenging and complex projects.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are Foundational.

Foundational is an early-stage, mission-driven startup solving one of the hardest engineering challenges of the decade: creating an exquisite, real-time understanding of Earth and orbit using ground-based lasers.

We aren't just gathering data; we are building the platform that will enable the next generation of space traffic control, resilient navigation, and Earth observation. - essential for the sustainability and resilience of the space domain, monitoring our climate and empowering the future economy.

This mission, this technology... will be foundational to the future of civilization.

What You'll Be Doing:

 - Designing, developing, and deploying scalable and robust SaaS applications using Python, React, FastAPI, MongoDB, modern AWS cloud services, Terraform, and Airflow.

 - Building and maintaining both the frontend and backend of our core product, ensuring seamless user experiences and high performance.

 - Implementing DevOps practices to streamline development workflows, automate deployments, and ensure continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) via Gitlab.

 - Collaborating closely with the product team to translate user needs and business requirements into innovative and user-friendly features.

 - Writing clean, maintainable, and well-documented code, adhering to best practices and industry standards.

 - Troubleshooting and resolving technical issues, ensuring the smooth operation of our platform.

 - Staying up-to-date with the latest technologies and trends in web development, DevOps, and the space industry.

 - Growing with us! As an early team member, you'll have the opportunity to contribute to all aspects of the development process and make a real impact on our product and company culture.
   
   

What We're Looking For:

 - Proven experience as a Full Stack Developer, with a strong portfolio showcasing your skills in both frontend and backend development.

 - Solid understanding of DevOps principles and experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)

 - Proficiency in front end e.g., React, Angular, Vue.js and frameworks such as e.g., Node.js or FastAPI.

 - Experience with databases such as MongoDB and MySQL.

 - Familiarity with API design and development, including serverless technologies, e.g. AWS ECS/API Gateway/Lambda.

 - A passion for space technology and a genuine interest in the space sector (desirable, but not essential).

 - A proactive, problem-solving attitude and a desire to learn and grow in a fast-paced startup environment.

 - Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

 - The ability to work independently and take ownership of your projects.

 - A strong desire to make a tangible difference and help build something extraordinary.
   
   

Bonus Points (We'd love it if you have...)

 - Experience with containerisation technologies, e.g. Docker, Kubernetes.

 - Contributions to open-source projects.

 - Familiarity with space situational awareness, space domain awareness or other ground-based space sensors (optical, RF, etc.)


What We Offer

 - A high-impact role with genuine ownership and autonomy.

 - EMI options in a fast-growing VC backed startup in the spacetech industry

 - Generous compensation and pension contributions

 - Offices in central London right in the heart of Borough and London Bridge with snacks, tea, coffee and regular socials.

source_jobs.title: Electro-Optical Engineer (Space Systems)
source_jobs.description:
Electro-Optical Engineer (Space Systems)

We are Foundational.



We're an early-stage, mission-driven startup tackling one of the most exciting and complex challenges today to create the most precise understanding of space and earth using ground-based lasers.

We are developing next-generation technologies for Satellite Laser Ranging (SLR) and Space Situational Awareness (SSA). Our products combine advanced optics, photonics, electronics, and space systems engineering to create novel capabilities for tracking and characterising objects in orbit.

As a growing startup, we work on technically challenging problems and rapidly transform new ideas into operational products.



Position Overview

We are seeking an Electro-Optical Engineer to lead the development of optical and photonic subsystems that form the core of our SLR and SSA products. The role will primarily be focused on space hardware but may also feature work on the ground segment of the system as required.

This role is focused on the design, development, integration, and testing of laser-based and optical sensing systems, with particular emphasis on electronics, optical architectures, and instrument performance.

You will take responsibility for projects from early concept development through detailed design, laboratory validation, qualification, and deployment. The role requires a hands-on engineer who is equally comfortable modelling an optical system, designing supporting electronics, and troubleshooting hardware in the lab.



Key Responsibilities

 - Design and develop optical and electro-optical subsystems for satellite laser ranging and space situational awareness applications.

 - Develop optical architectures incorporating lasers, detectors, filters, lenses, mirrors, retroreflectors, and associated opto-mechanical assemblies.

 - Support design and specification of supporting analogue and mixed-signal electronics for detector readout, timing, control, and data acquisition.

 - Perform optical performance modelling, signal-to-noise analysis, stray light analysis, and error budgeting.

 - Define subsystem requirements and verification approaches.

 - Lead laboratory integration, alignment, characterisation, and performance testing activities.

 - Support environmental testing and qualification activities for space hardware.

 - Work closely with systems, software, and mechanical engineers to deliver integrated products.

 - Contribute to the development of new sensing concepts and product opportunities within the SSA domain.



Required Qualifications

Please note that even if not all of these are met, candidates with the right mindset are still encouraged to apply. This includes being keen to work in a start up, lead your own projects, and owning development of a product from concept to qualification.

 - Several years of experience developing electro-optical or photonic systems from concept through testing and validation.

 - Strong understanding of optical system design and performance analysis.

 - Experience working with lasers, photodetectors, imaging sensors, or precision optical instrumentation.

 - Experience designing and testing analogue or mixed-signal electronics associated with optical systems.

 - Strong laboratory skills, including optical alignment, system integration, and troubleshooting.

 - Ability to independently own technical work packages and drive them to completion.
   
   

Preferred Skills

 - Experience working with flight hardware and/or qualifying flight hardware for space applications

 - Degree in Physics, Optical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Photonics, or a related discipline.

 - Experience with laser ranging, lidar, optical communications, remote sensing, astronomy instrumentation, or precision metrology systems.

 - Familiarity with single-photon detectors, avalanche photodiodes (APDs), SPADs, PMTs, or time-correlated measurement systems.

 - Experience with optical design and modelling tools such as Zemax OpticStudi

notes: Early-stage startup with a strong focus on engineering and technical roles.
